calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Quash And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Quash
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Mable Ding's Motion To Quash is Off calendar. On September 11, 2014, a request for dismissal of the entire action was filed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLES GLAUBERMAN, TEL. NO. (408) 677-5823 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CLAIRE JOHNSON RABA OF THE BAY AREA LEGAL AID, TEL. NO. 982-1300 X 6344 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT MABLE DING's MOTION to SET ASIDE JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO(CCP sec. 473(d).) IS GRANTED. The default and default judgment are set aside. Defendant has established that she was never served with the summons and the complaint, and therefore, the Court never obtained personal jurisdiction. (See CCP sec. 410.50; Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439, "In the absence of a voluntary submission to the authority of the court, compliance with the statutes governing service of process is essential to establish that court's personal jurisdiction over a defendant.") "When a court lacks jurisdiction in a fundamental sense, an ensuing judgment is void, and thus vulnerable to direct or collateral attack at any time." (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
